learned_facts.fact: Backup drive partitioning: separate partitions for each source drive prevents one backup from consuming all space. Recommended layout for dedicated backup HDD: Partition 1 (200-300GB NTFS) for wbadmin system images of C:, Partition 2 (remainder NTFS) for Robocopy mirror of G:. Use NTFS not ReFS -- ReFS lacks consumer tool support, no EFS encryption, limited compatibility with recovery environments as of 2026.

Backup scheduling best practice: Weekly full system image (wbadmin) Sunday night, Daily Robocopy incremental mirror Mon-Sat 2AM, Monthly full backup rotated to offsite removable HDD. Differential/full backups are safer for home users than long incremental chains. Task Scheduler: run as SYSTEM, highest privileges, start-when-available for missed runs, 4hr execution time limit.

Removable HDD best practices: Grandfather-Father-Son (GFS) rotation -- minimum 2 drives, swap weekly or monthly. Encrypt with BitLocker To Go (built into Win11 Pro). Label drives clearly: BACKUP-A [C+G] 2026-01 OFFSITE. Store in padded antistatic cases, stable temperature. Never store encryption recovery key with the physical drive. Power up and verify readability every 6-12 months.

Robocopy backup flags verified: robocopy SOURCE DEST /MIR /R:3 /W:5 /MT:8 /XJ /FFT /NP /LOG+:logfile. /MIR=mirror (delete removed), /R:3=retry 3x (default 1M!), /W:5=wait 5s between retries, /MT:8=threads (fewer for spinning HDD), /XJ=exclude junctions (avoid loops), /FFT=FAT time tolerance. Exit codes: 0=no change, 1=copied, 2=extras, 4=mismatches, 8+=error.

RyansRig storage layout for backup planning: C: Samsung 980 PRO 2TB NVMe (OS, 1863GB), D: Toshiba HDWQ140 4TB HDD (backup target, 3726GB), G: Samsung 990 EVO Plus 4TB NVMe (projects/BOSS, 3726GB). D: has ample capacity to hold both C: system images (~200-300GB per image) and G: Robocopy mirror (~2.2TB used). Removable HDDs available for offsite rotation.

80 Plus Gold PSU efficiency: requires minimum 90% efficiency at 50% load (peak efficiency point), 87% at 20% load, and 87% at 100% load for 115V systems. 80 Plus Platinum requires 92% at 50% load. Do not confuse Gold (90%) with Platinum (92%).

Intel VT-x provides hardware-assisted CPU virtualization with separate privilege rings (VMX root/non-root modes) for guests. It does NOT provide memory encryption (that is AMD SEV/Intel TME), GPU passthrough (that requires VT-d/IOMMU), or network I/O virtualization (that is VT-c/SR-IOV).

Standard ATX motherboard dimensions: 305mm x 244mm. This is the LARGEST standard consumer form factor. E-ATX (up to 330mm wide) is extended/enthusiast, not standard consumer. Micro-ATX is 244mm x 244mm (square). Mini-ITX is 170mm x 170mm. When asked about 305mm x 244mm, the answer is ATX.

PCIe bandwidth per generation (unidirectional, x16): PCIe 3.0 = ~16 GB/s, PCIe 4.0 = ~32 GB/s, PCIe 5.0 = ~64 GB/s, PCIe 6.0 = ~128 GB/s. Each generation doubles. Per lane: PCIe 4.0 = ~2 GB/s/lane, so x4 NVMe = ~8 GB/s unidirectional. Always distinguish unidirectional vs bidirectional (bidirectional = 2x unidirectional).

DDR5 bandwidth calculation: DDR5-6000 = 6000 MT/s x 8 bytes (64-bit bus) = 48 GB/s per channel. Dual-channel = 96 GB/s total. Each DDR5 DIMM has two 32-bit sub-channels internally, but dual-channel refers to two DIMMs on separate memory channels. Do not double-count sub-channels with dual-channel.

VRAM estimation for LLM inference at FP16: Parameters (billions) x 2 GB for weights, plus 10-20% overhead for KV cache and activations. A 7B model needs ~14 GB for weights + ~2-3 GB overhead = ~16-17 GB total. The formula is NOT params x 0.5 or params x 4.

GDDR6X achieves higher bandwidth than GDDR6 through PAM4 signaling (Pulse Amplitude Modulation with 4 levels), encoding 2 bits per signal transition instead of 1 (NRZ). It does NOT use a wider bus or higher voltage. RTX 4090 bandwidth: 384-bit bus x 21 Gbps = (384/8) x 21 = 1,008 GB/s.

Quad-channel memory configuration: requires exactly 4 DIMMs, one per channel, installed in the correct color-coded slots on a quad-channel motherboard. DIMMs must match capacity, speed, and rank. It is NOT 8 DIMMs with 4 matched pairs. Any 4 slots is wrong; must be the designated quad-channel slots.
